citation.anchor: During TLB inserts, the region identifier from the select region register is used to tag translations

preconditions: Execution is at CPL0 with instruction translation disabled
Two data translations use the same region-zero virtual page and distinct physical pages
inputs: Two distinct 18-bit RID values written to RR0
Two ITC.D insertions at the same virtual address, one under each RID
A data serialization after each dependent RR write
expected_results: Both translations coexist because their RID tags differ
Selecting the first RID loads only the first physical value
Selecting the second RID loads only the second physical value
allowed_results: No diagnostic interruption remains after both translated loads
cases.committed_prefix: Each ITC.D insertion commits under the RID selected at insertion time; each serialized RR write selects the matching context before its load
cases.restart_action: No restart occurs for either successful translated load

oracle.derivation: The two RIDs, virtual address, physical pages, data values, insertion order, and expected selection are test-owned literals derived from the cited RID tagging rule.

limitations: Cross-region VHPT hashing and purge-by-RID behavior are separate closure units

citation.anchor: insertable_pages returns the page sizes that are supported for TLB insertions and region registers

preconditions: The PAL static firmware gate is available
The product page-size profile is fixed by the architected Table 4-5 sizes
inputs: Each reserved argument independently set to one
expected_results: The valid call returns exactly ten insertable page-size bits
The purge mask contains those ten sizes plus 4 GiB
Each nonzero reserved argument returns -2 with zero payloads
allowed_results: No alternative page-size profile is permitted for this declared synthetic CPU
required_unchanged_state: Guest memory and unrelated architectural state
cases.committed_prefix: A valid call publishes both complete masks; invalid arguments publish only status -2
cases.restart_action: Each PAL call returns directly without retry

oracle.derivation: The procedure index, bitmap convention, ten insertable exponents, additional purge exponent, and invalid-argument status are test-owned literals from the cited PAL and Table 4-5 contracts.

limitations: PAL_VM_INFO geometry is a separate discovery contract

citation.anchor: For region registers, bits {63:61} are used. The remainder of the bits are ignored

preconditions: Execution is at CPL0 with NaT-clear selector and value GRs
Instruction and data serialization follow the complete write sequence
inputs: Write selectors with poisoned low 61 bits
Read selectors with different poisoned low 61 bits
expected_results: RR0 through RR7 retain eight distinct images without aliasing
Only selector bits 63:61 choose the register
Both move directions execute through the typed system plane
allowed_results: No exception remains after the legal selector traversal
required_unchanged_state: Guest code and unrelated architectural state
cases.committed_prefix: All eight writes commit before instruction and data serialization; delayed reads observe exactly those values
cases.restart_action: No restart occurs for legal CPL0 RR accesses

oracle.derivation: The three-bit region selector, eight-register count, ignored-bit poisons, and distinct images are test-owned constants from the cited instruction and RR contracts.

limitations: RR field validity and translation effects are claimed by separate atomic rows

citation.anchor: Region Identifier (rid) bits 0 through 17 must be implemented on all processor models

preconditions: Execution is at CPL0 with PSR.ic enabled
A General Exception handler validates ISR.code 0x30 and resumes after each rejected write
inputs: VE, every one of the 18 product RID bits, and all ten insertable ps encodings
expected_results: Every implemented field and page size reads back exactly
All 39 reserved singleton bits and 54 unsupported page sizes fault once
Every rejected write leaves the RR7 sentinel unchanged
allowed_results: The diagnostic record may retain the final delivered General Exception
expected_faults: Reserved Register/Field for every reserved bit and unsupported preferred page size
cases.committed_prefix: Legal writes commit after serialization; the 93 rejected writes commit only handler counters
cases.restart_action: Validate ISR.code 0x30, advance CR.IIP one bundle, and RFI after each rejected write

result_observations: sentinel, ISR, and value mismatch counters

oracle.derivation: The VE/ps/RID positions, 18-bit product RID width, ten page-size exponents, reserved ranges, exact fault count, and serialization requirements are test-owned literals from the cited RR and page-size contracts.

limitations: The product implements the mandatory 18 RID bits; larger implementation-dependent RID widths are outside this profile
Migration rejection is supporting E1 evidence until paired with an E2 migration boundary
